risks: Real upstream model licenses and terms still need re-checking immediately before any actual training launch.
Qwen compatibility is metadata-only; tokenizer, serving, tool-call, structured-output, quantization, and memory behavior still need runtime receipts.
Remote CI is failing on a pre-existing non-model schema export duplication in release_check.sh after unit tests pass; Goal 4 did not modify harness schema registration.
The sample dataset manifest is a local dry-run stub and not a production dataset registry entry.
Adapter, eval, and promotion link slots are present but await real downstream artifacts.
Serving-probe receipt is metadata-only and intentionally does not verify endpoint health, chat completions, tool calls, structured outputs, runtime context, or memory behavior.
Remote CI may still fail on the pre-existing non-model schema export duplication in release_check.sh unless another lane fixes harness schema registration.
PR #15 cannot be landed by this worker without supervisor integration because current origin/main conflicts with Goal 4 in shared autonomy state plus CLI/validation/schema surfaces.
